DAC_Back_Link_Usability_01 (AAA)


Description

Summary
Move the “Back” link outside the main content region so it aligns with Welcome to GOV.UK  Design System guidance and expected screen reader behaviour.

Description
On the Privacy Notice – Create a UK catch certificate page, the Back link has been placed inside the main content region.

This implementation is inconsistent with the Welcome to GOV.UK  Design System and negatively impacts screen reader users. When a screen reader user activates the “Skip to main content” link, they would expect the first element within the main content region to be the H1 page heading. Instead, the Back link is currently encountered first, which breaks expected navigation patterns and consistency across Welcome to GOV.UK  services.

This issue may also be present on other pages within the service and should be fixed wherever it occurs.

URL
https://fes-ext.pre.private.defra.gov.uk/create-catch-certificate/privacy-notice 

Journey
Journey 1, Step 3

Current Behaviour
The Back link appears inside the main content region.

Screen reader users encounter the Back link before the H1 when skipping to main content.

This is inconsistent with Welcome to GOV.UK  standards and other Welcome to GOV.UK  services.

Expected Behaviour
The Back link is placed outside of the main content region.

When a user activates “Skip to main content”, focus lands directly on the H1 heading.

Page structure is consistent with Welcome to GOV.UK  Design System guidance and user expectations.

Solution
Move the Back link so it appears before the main landmark, in line with the Welcome to GOV.UK  Design System – Back link component guidance.

Acceptance Criteria
The Back link is rendered outside the main content region

The first element inside main is the H1 page heading

Activating “Skip to main content” moves focus directly to the H1

No visual regression is introduced

The change is applied consistently across all affected pages in the service
